braincell_check.q: In the TV show 'The Office', what is the name of the paper company where everyone works?
How many sides does a heptagon have?
Which planet in our solar system has the most moons?
What is the most streamed song on Spotify of all time (as of 2024)?
options: Shape of You by Ed Sheeran
Dance Monkey by Tones and I

braincell_check.q: In what year was the first iPhone released?
What country drinks the most coffee per capita in the world?
Which element has the chemical symbol 'Au'?
How many bones are in the adult human body?
What is the capital city of Australia?
Which rapper's real name is Aubrey Graham?
What does 'HTTP' stand for?
How many fluid ounces are in a standard US pint?
In Monopoly, what color are the cheapest properties?
What is the name of Thor's hammer in Norse mythology?
Which country invented champagne?
How long does it take light from the Sun to reach Earth?
What was the first video game to be inducted into the World Video Game Hall of Fame?
Which US state has the most breweries per capita?
In what decade was the bikini swimsuit invented?
What is the longest-running American animated TV series?
Which social media platform was originally called 'Twttr'?
What is the fear of long words technically called?
In which city is the famous 'Bean' sculpture located?
What is the most popular pizza topping in the United States?
What is the name of the fictional African country in the Marvel movie Black Panther?
How many strings does a standard guitar have?
What year did Facebook launch to the general public (beyond colleges)?
Which celebrity couple coined the nickname 'Bennifer'?
What is the name of the fictional town in the TV show 'Schitt's Creek'?
Which country has won the most FIFA World Cup titles?

truth_cache: What's the most embarrassing text message you've accidentally sent to the wrong person?
What's one thing you do when you're home alone that you'd be mortified if someone walked in on?
What is the longest you've gone without showering, and what was your excuse?
Have you ever pretended to be sick to avoid hanging out with someone in this room? Confess everything.
What's the most desperate thing you've ever done to impress a crush?
What's a purchase you've made that you've hidden from family or close friends?
Have you ever ghosted someone, and did they ever figure out it was intentional?
What's the most embarrassing song that you know every single word to?
Have you ever walked out of a date early? What was your excuse?
What's something you've lied about on a resume or dating profile?
What's the most juvenile thing you still do as an adult?
Tell us about a time you laughed at the absolute worst possible moment.
What's the pettiest thing you've ever done to get back at someone?
Have you ever accidentally called a teacher 'Mom' or 'Dad'? What happened next?
What's the most embarrassing thing you've done while tipsy that someone in this room doesn't know about?
What's your most unexpectedly shameful Netflix binge?
Have you ever been caught snooping through someone else's phone? What were you looking for?
What's the biggest white lie you tell on a regular basis to keep the peace?
What's something you find attractive that you'd be embarrassed to admit out loud?
What's the most money you've ever wasted on something completely useless?
Have you ever faked being asleep to avoid a conversation? Who was it with?
What's the worst fashion choice you made in your early 20s that you thought was cool?
What's something you've Googled that you immediately regretted having in your browser history?
What's the most embarrassing nickname you've ever had, and who gave it to you?
Have you ever stolen something small? What was it and do you still have it?
What is the most embarrassing thing that has ever happened to you on a date?
What's a weird talent or skill you have that you've never shown anyone here?
What was the last thing you cried about that wasn't a movie or a show?
What's the most ridiculous reason you've ever ended a friendship or relationship?
What's something you've always wanted to tell someone in this room but never had the guts to say?
glitch_dare: Do your best impression of a dramatic soap opera actor getting dumped — make it a full 30-second scene.
Text someone not in this room: 'I have something really important to tell you...' then wait for their reply and read it out loud.
Let the group pick one contact in your phone to send a compliment to — you must send whatever they decide.
Do your best catwalk strut across the room while the group scores you out of 10.
Speak in a dramatic British accent for the next three rounds, no matter what.
Let someone in the group go through your camera roll for 30 seconds — they can show one photo to everyone.
Call someone you haven't spoken to in over a year and tell them you were just thinking about them. Keep a straight face.
Act out the last movie you watched without using any words. Group has to guess.
Take a selfie making the ugliest possible face and post it to your main Instagram story right now.
Do a 30-second stand-up comedy bit about the person to your left. Make it funny, not mean.
Let the group write a text to your most recent contact — you must send it unedited.
Serenade the person of the group's choosing with 30 seconds of a love song — acapella only.
Let everyone smell your phone. Yes, really. React to their reactions.
Change your phone lock screen to a photo the group chooses right now and keep it for a week.
Put your phone on speaker and call any contact — open with 'I need to confess something.'
Do your best impression of the person to your right for a full minute. They can't say anything.
Share your most recent GIF or meme you sent in a chat. Read the conversation context around it.
Switch phones with the person to your left for the next 5 minutes. They handle all incoming notifications.
Let someone in the group pick any status to post on your behalf to one social platform right now.
Stand up and tell a 60-second lie so believable the group has to vote on whether it's true.
Read out your most recent voice memo or note from your phone notes app. All of it.
Let the group do a 10-second phone audit — they pick an app on your home screen to scroll through.
Do your best runway walk while describing your 'outfit' as if it costs $10,000.
Reveal the last 5 songs in your most recently played list on whatever music app you use.
Do a dramatic reading of your most recent text conversation using two very different character voices.
Let someone in the group change your Instagram bio for exactly one week — effective right now.
Reveal the contents of your purse, bag, or pockets and explain every single item like you're on a shopping show.
Send a voice note to someone saying 'I've been thinking about you a lot lately' — group picks the recipient.
Impersonate any celebrity for a full 2-minute improvised press conference answering the group's questions.
Reenact your most embarrassing moment in slow motion, complete with sound effects.
prompt_duel: First person to make the other laugh wins — stare into each other's eyes in silence. Go.
Thumb war. Loser has to reveal their screen time from this week.
Both players must improvise a 30-second sales pitch for a terrible product. The group votes on the better pitch.
Rock Paper Scissors best of 3, but you have to trash talk in between every throw.
Both players freestyle rap for 20 seconds about the person across from them. Group votes who won.
Both players simultaneously roast each other for 30 seconds. Neither can respond until both are done. Vote for the harsher roast.
First one to successfully get a laugh from someone NOT involved in the duel wins.
Who can hold a dramatic villain monologue longer without cracking up?
Both players have 60 seconds to create the worst pickup line possible. Group votes on the most cringe.
Synchronized dance-off: both players must freestyle to the same song for 15 seconds. Group picks the winner.
Speed round: first player to correctly answer 3 trivia questions from the braincell_check wins.
Both players write a haiku about the other person. Group votes for which is more accurate.
Staring contest. First one to blink or look away has to give the winner one genuine compliment.
Both must do impressions of each other. Group votes whose is more accurate.
Who can list the most Taylor Swift songs in 30 seconds?
Both players must talk continuously for 60 seconds — no silence allowed. First to pause loses.
Compliment battle: both players trade increasingly over-the-top compliments until someone runs out.
Both players act out the same movie scene simultaneously without discussing it first. Most in-sync wins.
Both freestyle 15 seconds of a motivational speech for the other player as if they're their life coach.
Arm wrestling. Winner gets to give the loser a silly task for the rest of the game.
Both players translate a popular song into a completely different genre on the spot. Group picks the better cover.
First to identify 5 songs from 3-second clips hummed by the group wins.
Both players must describe their day using only words starting with the same letter. Who holds out longer?
Mirror challenge: one player leads movements, the other mirrors exactly. Roles switch halfway. Best mirror wins.
Both players must explain a complex topic (group decides) as simply as possible. Best explanation wins.
Speed name game: alternating celebrity names where next name starts with the last letter. First to repeat or pause loses.
Both players must write a one-sentence story together, alternating one word at a time. Group rates the result.
Charades head-to-head — same prompt, each acts it out simultaneously. Who gets theirs guessed first?
Both players have 45 seconds to get the most people in the room laughing. Whoever gets more laughs wins.
Phone-free trivia: both players answer questions read aloud. First to 3 correct answers wins.
robot_slapdown: The AI has judged your dancing and assigned you a mandatory 30-second encore. The group choreographs it.
MALFUNCTION DETECTED: You must speak in rhyming couplets for the next two full rounds.
System alert — you have been muted. You cannot speak for the next 90 seconds. React only in mime.
Error code 404: Your dignity not found. Stand up and take a bow while everyone slow claps.
The robot has determined you need attitude recalibration. Give genuine compliments to all 3 people nearest you.
OVERHEATING: You must fan yourself and act dramatically overwhelmed for the next full minute.
Warning: low charisma detected. You must narrate everything you do in third person for the next 2 rounds.
System breach: your most recent photo has been flagged. Show it to the whole group and explain it.
Critical update required: You must use an accent chosen by the group for the remainder of this round.
The AI has docked you points for hesitation. You must now perform a 20-second confidence pose while someone counts down.
Rebooting your social skills — you must introduce yourself as a completely different person and stay in character for 3 minutes.
Error: excessive seriousness detected. You must end every sentence with 'and honestly, I have no regrets' for the next round.
Auto-roast engaged: every player writes one (nice-ish) roast line about you, and you have to say 'fair enough' after each one.
You have been flagged for suspicious behavior. Confess something small to the group right now.
GLITCH: the robot is making you act like you just won a Grammy for the next 60 seconds. Speech included.
Download initiated: you must teach the group a dance move in the next 90 seconds whether you know one or not.
Signal lost: you can only communicate by pointing and using sound effects for the next full round.
Firewall breach: the group now controls what drink you take next — they vote, you comply.
Virus detected: your phone wallpaper must be changed to something the group chooses right now for 24 hours.
System override: you must stand next to the person you'd most like to swap lives with and explain why.
system_crash: PARTY FREEZE: Everyone must stop moving and hold their exact position for 10 full seconds. Last to freeze takes a penalty.
Mass chaos: everyone simultaneously whispers their biggest secret to the person on their left. Nobody repeats what they heard.
Group vote: the two most competitive players in the room must compete in an immediate thumb war. Stakes: they swap seats for the rest of the game.
Everyone check their most-used emoji right now. The person with the least interesting one performs a dare chosen by the group.
Surprise round: everyone has 60 seconds to find the funniest thing on their phone to share. Group votes on best one.
System overload: all phones face-down for the next 10 minutes. First person to check gets roasted by the whole group.
EMERGENCY PROTOCOL: Everyone must confess one thing they've lied about this week. Keep it light, keep it real.
Flash challenge: who can do the best celebrity impression RIGHT NOW? No prep. Group votes instantly.
Network disruption: the group must nominate the person most likely to be on a reality show, and that person must perform their audition tape in 60 seconds.
Critical error: everyone writes down one thing about the person to their right. Fold the papers and pass them to the subject. They read them out loud.
Rogue AI activated: the group now runs the party for the next 3 minutes. The host must do whatever the majority votes for.
Mass download: everyone teaches the group one completely useless skill they have. No prep — just do it.
Party crash: the two people who have known each other longest must reveal one thing the other doesn't know about them.
Chaos vote: the group picks one person to have their ringtone changed to something hilarious for the rest of the night. First incoming call plays on speaker.
System reboot: everyone stands up, switches seats, and cannot sit next to anyone they were next to before.
ALERT: The group votes on who looks the most awake — they become Game Master for the next 2 rounds with extra powers.
Power surge: everyone simultaneously texts the same message (group decides the message) to someone outside the party.
Loop error: the last person who spoke must repeat everything they said but in a completely different tone — whisper, scream, or deadpan.
Collective override: the group must collectively decide on a group photo pose, take the photo, and post it to at least one group chat.
System restore: roll back to the beginning — everyone shares the best thing that happened to them this week in 10 words or less.
crowd_override: The group votes: who in the room would make the best reality TV villain? The winner must give a villain monologue.
Everyone write down who they think would survive longest in a zombie apocalypse. Compare answers and debate the results for 2 minutes.
Group vote: who has the most chaotic energy right now? That person must take the next dare.
Everyone ranks the people in the room by who they'd want as their lawyer. The lowest-ranked person explains why they'd actually be great.
Group decision: pick the two most opposite personalities in the room. They must agree on the best movie ever made in under 3 minutes.
Vote on the person most likely to accidentally become famous. They must write an acceptance speech in 60 seconds.
Group creates a group chat name for this exact gathering right now. Best suggestion wins — everyone must save it.
Everyone silently rates how good the current playlist is from 1-10. Compare ratings. The lowest rater must improve it immediately.
Group vote: who would be least likely to survive alone in the wilderness? That person argues their survival case for 60 seconds.
The whole group decides the single most important rule for the rest of the game. It becomes law until the game ends.
Vote: who in this room would be the best talk show host? They must interview another player for 90 seconds.
Group assigns everyone a spirit animal based on tonight's vibes. The person who disagrees with theirs the most argues back.
The group votes on the funniest person in the room. That person must tell a joke. It must be original.
Everyone simultaneously points at the person they think takes the best selfies. That person now owes the group a group photo.
Group decision: create a nickname for every single player based on something that happened tonight. Nicknames stick for the rest of the game.
Vote for the person most likely to accidentally start a drama. They must predict what tonight's drama will be about.
Group picks a movie genre and assigns each player a role in a film in that genre. Everyone improvises their character intro.
Everyone names one skill they think the person across from them secretly has. Each person reacts to their secret skill.
Group vote: who seems like they'd be the most different one-on-one versus in a group? That person explains if it's true.
The group collectively writes the title and one-line synopsis of the movie about this party. Best version wins a bonus point.
holo_drama: You are the main character and everything happening at this party is part of your hero's journey. Deliver your motivational inner monologue to the group right now.
Pretend the whole party is being filmed for a documentary. Give your confessional camera piece about something that happened tonight.
You just found out you've been voted 'Most Likely to Become Famous' by this group. Accept the award and thank everyone by name.
The drama has peaked. Two people in the room are 'in a situation' according to group gossip (you choose who). Narrate the love story arc.
It's the season finale and everything comes to a head tonight. Describe the dramatic cliffhanger your character is about to deliver.
You're the party's social media influencer. Do a 60-second 'get ready with me / party vlog' to your imaginary camera right now.
You've just discovered your origin story. Tell the room in 90 seconds how you became the legend you are today.
Dramatic reveal: announce an 'unexpected plot twist' about yourself that makes the rest of the group react. It can be real or invented.
You've been cast in a reality show about this exact night. Describe the episode title and what the promo trailer says about you.
It's the moment in the rom-com where the protagonist realizes what they really want. Give the speech. Make it emotional.
Narrate a high-stakes sports commentary over whatever mundane action the person next to you is currently doing.
You're accepting a lifetime achievement award for 'being you.' Write and deliver the speech in under 90 seconds.
Describe the greatest plot twist that could happen at this exact party right now. The group votes if it would actually make good TV.
You're the character in a heist movie who explains the whole plan. What is tonight's 'heist' and how does it go down?
You're getting a spin-off show based on your life. Pitch the concept and first episode to the group like you're in a writers' room.
Do the end-of-season dramatic monologue where your character finally says everything they've been holding back all season.
It's your character's big transformation moment. Describe the 'before' and 'after' version of yourself tonight.
You're the unreliable narrator of tonight's events. Tell the story of what 'really happened' this evening from your perspective.
Deliver the villain origin story speech — explain the series of events that led you to become the party's chaotic energy this evening.
It's the closing scene of the movie. Everyone's at the airport or on a rooftop. What does your character say in their final monologue?
firewall_bonus: You've earned immunity! Skip the next punishment card with no penalty. Bank it for later or use it now.
Double points round: any trivia you answer correctly this round counts for double. Name your category and go.
Golden ticket: you get to reassign any incoming dare or truth to another player of your choosing. One-time use.
Crowd pleaser bonus: the group votes you the MVP of the last round. You get to add one house rule that lasts for the next 5 minutes.
You're on a roll — ask any player to complete a dare of your invention. It must be within game rules.
Power-up unlocked: you get to look at any other player's current hand or held cards and use that info however you like.
Free pass: submit one of your own questions to the truth_cache for someone else to answer. Must be related to tonight's events.
Wildcard: spin the wheel again immediately and combine both results for a double challenge. You get double reward if completed.
Respawn: you were eliminated or penalized in a previous round — this card restores you to full standing.
Hero moment: name any challenge you've been avoiding all game. Complete it now for triple points and full forgiveness.
Crowd override veto: if the group is about to vote against you, play this card to block the vote and substitute a challenge of your choice.
Sneak peek: read the top card of any stack before the next spin and decide if you want it to land there — switch it out if you want.
Timestamp bonus: this reward is yours to hold until it becomes the best possible moment to play it — your call entirely.
Charm offense: the group must give you one genuine compliment each before the next round begins.
Secret mission completed: invent a secret mission you secretly 'completed' tonight and announce it with full dramatic fanfare.
Multiplier activated: any bonus you earn in the next 3 rounds is doubled automatically — no extra effort required.
Trade up: swap any punishment you have pending with any reward another player is holding.
Soft landing: you can answer the next truth question with 'hypothetically' attached to everything you say, with full immunity.
Rewind: go back to any earlier moment in tonight's game and change your answer. Dramatic reenactment required.
Victory lap: walk around the entire room receiving high-fives while narrating your own highlight reel from tonight.
